patterns_recognized: Same-series doubling: 4 of 6 GOLD picks (TB@BOS, COL@PHI, DET@KC, NYY@MIL) are exact repeats of yesterday's GOLD losses — model doesn't adjust for within-series momentum after a single game.
Small-sample SP asterisk recurs: F1_xera_gap* appears again on NYY@MIL and CHC@TEX; yesterday NYY@MIL's asterisked F1 preceded a GOLD loss to a small-sample MIL starter.
Negative-edge GOLD slippage: COL@PHI carries edge -9.52pp (below -8pp DOWNGRADE threshold) yet remains GOLD-tiered — the -8pp cap is blocking stake but not tier assignment.

Home-favorite overconfidence: SEA@CHW (74% yesterday, lost 12-8) and NYM@ARI (68% yesterday, lost) are today's clearest recurrence risk; both matchups repeat with model still on the previously-wrong side.

LAA @ TOR.reasoning: Edge +2.77pp falls below the 4pp minimum — correct SKIP. More concerning: TOR's Stage 1 (F5) win probability is only 40.47% while Stage 2 reads 67.43%, a 27pp gap indicating the pick is entirely a bullpen/late-inning thesis. Calibrated probability ~57% from 67.43% raw (0.6-0.7 bin). Yesterday TOR won cleanly behind Cease; today's F5 weakness and low edge give no actionable lift. Confidence_downgrade already flagged.

OAK @ BAL.reasoning: OAK picked at 57.03% while Vegas prices BAL at 53.58% (fair 46.42%). Edge +10.61pp is in the valid band but grader assigned SKIP. Stage 1/2 discordance: OAK wins only 44.44% of F5s yet leads in the full game — partial bullpen thesis. Yesterday the model picked BAL in this series and lost 4-3 to OAK, suggesting OAK quality is real, but insufficient positive-tier signals to elevate from SKIP.

HOU @ CIN.reasoning: HOU won 10-0 yesterday; today the model again likes HOU (52.53% full game) with a massive F5 advantage (85.3%). But fair_prob=38.5% means Vegas strongly favors CIN, likely reflecting the acute_roster stress flag (possible CIN lineup/SP adjustment). Stage 1/2 gap of 0.33 means this is purely an early-innings HOU advantage — full-game conviction is thin. Fair_prob threshold filter correctly triggers SKIP.

TB @ BOS.reasoning: Yesterday's exact matchup: GOLD-tier TB pick, F3_swing_take_gap=762, lost 0-2 to BOS. Today: same setup, F3=911.8 (stronger signal, same failure pattern). Calibrated TB probability ~52% from 54.93% raw. BOS SP held TB scoreless yesterday; today's starter is different but BOS pitching depth remains. No new information breaks the same-series-loss pattern. F3 is a real signal but it lost in this exact context 24 hours ago.

WSH @ MIA.reasoning: WSH picked at 52% vs Vegas pricing MIA at ~61% (fair 39.02%). Model flipped from MIA (yesterday's pick, lost 2-3) to WSH today. Stage 1/2: WSH F5=60.4% vs full=52.0% — F5 conviction doesn't carry to the full game. Fair_prob threshold (39.02% < 42%) correctly triggers SKIP. Cannot verify SP change that might justify the direction flip without live data.

COL @ PHI.reasoning: Yesterday COL beat PHI 9-7 in this same matchup — GOLD PHI pick lost. Today model again picks PHI at 55% with edge -9.52pp, breaching the -8pp DOWNGRADE threshold (Heuristic #2). Vegas prices PHI at 64.52%; the model is contrarian against a strong market favorite. PHI bullpen flagged for fatigue (stress_warnings=bullpen_fatigue). Confidence_downgrade=True. Three simultaneous red flags: negative edge beyond the cap, fatigue, direct series loss.

MIN @ CLE.reasoning: CLE picked at 58.89% with near-perfect Stage 1/2 agreement (F5: 59.07%, full: 58.89%). Edge +6.49pp valid, but grader assigned SKIP. Yesterday CLE won 6-4 in this same series; however, bullpen_fatigue stress warning flags that CLE's relievers who carried yesterday may be overextended. Confidence_downgrade=True. SKIP is appropriate; direction is plausible but carry risk is real.

CHC @ TEX.reasoning: CHC beat TEX 7-1 yesterday with nearly identical signals (F2=0.023 yesterday vs 0.025 today, F1 also present). The direction is supported by independent F2 lineup evidence. F1_xera_gap=0.80* carries the asterisk (small-sample SP) — a concern mitigated by F2 firing independently. Edge -3.16pp keeps stake_mult=0, so no staking recommended. Confirming direction and GOLD tier; conviction limited by negative edge and confidence_downgrade=True.

DET @ KC.reasoning: Yesterday DET @ KC was a GOLD LOSS (DET 51.3%, F2=0.021, lost 3-4 in a one-run game). Today: same matchup, same F2_xwoba_gap=0.021, same DET probability 51.33%. Acute_roster stress warning raises concern about DET's lineup today. DET's F5 probability (40.87%) indicates KC is superior in the early innings — the full-game DET pick lives entirely in the late innings. Repeating the same signal on the same matchup after a direct series loss warrants DOWNGRADE.

SEA @ CHW.reasoning: Yesterday the model's worst loss: CHW picked at 74%, SEA won 12-8. Today CHW again at 54.89% (calibrated ~52%). The model retains a CHW home-field preference after yesterday's blowout. SEA's 12-run output indicates real offensive strength. Already SKIP (tier assignment). Confirming SKIP is correct; if this were actionable the SEA direction would have stronger recent backing, but a SKIP-vs-SKIP distinction is non-actionable.

NYY @ MIL.reasoning: Yesterday NYY @ MIL was a GOLD LOSS — Misiorowski (small-sample) shut out NYY, exactly matching the rookie SP blindspot (Heuristic #3). Today F1_xera_gap=1.68* again carries the asterisk, indicating today's MIL starter comparison is also small-sample. NYY offense noted as cold in yesterday's postgame (3rd-straight low-run game). Bullpen_fatigue stress and negative edge -2.96pp add to concerns. Four converging risk signals on a recurring pattern.

NYM @ ARI.reasoning: ARI at 67.43% while Vegas is near-coin-flip at 49.25% (fair) — edge +18.18pp exceeds the 15pp SKIP ceiling. Yesterday identical setup (ARI 68%, lost 1-3). Calibrated probability only ~57% (0.6-0.7 bin). Heuristic #1: home-favorite overconfidence at >65% with two such losses yesterday (SEA@CHW, NYM@ARI). SKIP is correctly applied; the direction may be right but the model's stated confidence is not market-supported.

STL @ SD.reasoning: STL picked at 54.33% full game but only 33.58% in F5 — a 20.75pp Stage 1/2 gap, the largest on today's slate. Heuristic #5: this is a pure bullpen/late-leverage pick; STL SP is likely a significant underdog. Fair_prob=42.13% confirms Vegas heavily favors SD. Yesterday the model picked SD and lost 0-6; today it flips to STL without live data confirming STL bullpen rest. Cannot verify late-game thesis without bullpen availability data.

PIT @ SF.reasoning: Cleanest GOLD pick on today's slate: Stage 1 (PIT F5=57.81%) and Stage 2 (full=59.99%) agree within 2.18pp — no bullpen-carry concern. Edge +8.79pp in the valid [4,15]pp band. F2_xwoba_gap=0.031 is the strongest F2 signal among today's GOLD picks. Shadow model (56.7%) confirms direction. No stress warnings, confidence_downgrade=False. Not a series repeat from yesterday. Calibrated ~52% from 60% raw, but signal convergence and clean edge justify maintaining GOLD.

ATL @ LAD.reasoning: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO signals an unreliable SP comparison today, despite yesterday's clean LAD win 3-1. Edge -11.63pp: model gives LAD only 52.92% vs Vegas's 64.55% — model significantly underrates LAD relative to the market. Bullpen_fatigue stress. The HARD_VETO plus negative edge plus fatigue are three independent reasons to SKIP. The model's own reduced confidence (52.92% vs yesterday's 64%) reflects the unstable SP situation.
